title: Missing Semicolon
description: Find the line with the syntax error.
explanation: Missing semicolon at the end of the variable declaration 'int x = 10'.

title: Wrong Format Specifier
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Using '%d' to print a float. Should use '%f' instead.

title: Undeclared Variable
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Variable 'b' is used but never declared. Only 'a' is declared.

title: Missing Return Type
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Function 'add' is missing a return type. It should be 'int add(int a, int b)'.

title: Wrong Comparison Operator
description: Find the line with the logical error.
explanation: Using assignment '=' instead of comparison '==' in the if condition.

title: Missing Ampersand in scanf
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: scanf requires the address of the variable. Should be '&num' instead of 'num'.

title: Array Out of Bounds
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Array index 3 is out of bounds for an array of size 3. Valid indices are 0, 1, 2.

title: Missing Header
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: printf is used without including <stdio.h>. The header must be included.

title: Mismatched Braces
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The for loop's closing brace is missing. 'return 0;' is inside the loop and main's brace closes the for loop instead.

title: String Assignment Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Cannot assign a string to a char array using '='. Use strcpy(name, "Hello") instead.

title: Pointer Dereference Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Dereferencing an uninitialized pointer 'p' causes undefined behavior. It must point to a valid memory location first.

title: Wrong Loop Condition
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The loop uses 'i--' with condition 'i <= 10'. Since i starts at 0 and decrements, this creates an infinite loop.

title: Division by Zero
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Division by zero causes undefined behavior in C.

title: Missing Break in Switch
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Missing 'break' after case 1. Without it, execution falls through to case 2, printing both 'One' and 'Two'.

title: Sizeof Pointer vs Array
description: Find the line with the logical error.
explanation: When an array is passed to a function, it decays to a pointer. sizeof(arr) gives the pointer size, not the array size.

title: Misuse of Macro
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The macro expands to '2 + 3 * 2 + 3 = 11' instead of '(2+3)*(2+3) = 25'. Macro parameters should be parenthesized: ((x) * (x)).

title: Dangling Else
description: Find the line with the logical error.
explanation: The 'else' binds to the nearest 'if (y)', not 'if (x)'. This is the dangling else problem. Use braces to clarify intent.

title: Integer Overflow
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Adding 1 to INT_MAX causes signed integer overflow, which is undefined behavior in C.

title: Wrong Pointer Arithmetic
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: After 'p = p + 5', p points past the allocated memory. Dereferencing and writing to it is undefined behavior.

title: Return Local Array
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Returning a pointer to a local array. The array 'arr' is destroyed when the function returns, making the pointer dangling.

title: Missing Null Terminator
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The char array has no room for the null terminator '\0'. printf with '%s' reads past the array, causing undefined behavior.

title: Double Free Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Calling free() on the same pointer twice causes undefined behavior (double free).

title: Struct Member Access
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Using '.' operator on a pointer. Should use '->' operator: p->x = 10. Also p is uninitialized.

title: Enum Redefinition
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Redeclaring 'RED' as an int variable shadows the enum constant. This causes a compilation error in C.

title: Uninitialized Variable in Condition
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Variable 'x' is used without initialization. Its value is indeterminate, leading to undefined behavior.

title: Valid Variable Declaration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is a valid C program that declares an integer, prints it, and returns 0.

title: Character Array Initialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is correct. The string literal initializes the char array with an implicit null terminator.

title: Pointer to Integer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The pointer p stores the address of x, and *p correctly dereferences it to print 5.

title: Void Main
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: According to the C standard, main must return int, not void. 'void main()' is not standard-compliant.

title: For Loop Syntax
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid C99 code. Declaring 'int i' inside the for loop is allowed in C99 and later.

title: Invalid Array Size
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Variable Length Arrays (VLAs) are valid in C99. This code correctly declares and uses a VLA.

title: Typedef Usage
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. typedef creates an alias 'uint' for 'unsigned int'.

title: Comma Operator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The comma operator evaluates each expression and returns the last one. x will be 3.

title: Const Pointer Modification
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Assigning the address of a const int to a non-const pointer and modifying it is undefined behavior and violates const correctness.

title: Struct Initialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The struct is properly defined and initialized using an initializer list.

title: Nested Ternary
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nested ternary operators are valid in C. The expression correctly evaluates to 'positive'.

title: Static Variable Retention
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. Static local variables retain their value across function calls. Output: 1 2 3.

title: String Comparison with ==
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Using '==' compares addresses of the arrays, not the string contents. Use strcmp() for string comparison.

title: Recursive Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is a valid recursive factorial function. factorial(5) correctly returns 120.

title: Pointer to Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. pp is a pointer to a pointer. **pp correctly dereferences twice to get the value 10.

title: Implicit Int Conversion
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: While syntactically valid, 'a / b' performs integer division (result is 2, not 2.5). At least one operand must be cast to float.

title: Enum Default Values
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. Enums start at 0 by default, so WED equals 2. The output is 2.

title: Multi-dimensional Array
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The 2D array is properly initialized and mat[1][2] correctly accesses the value 6.

title: Bitwise AND
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. 6 (110) & 3 (011) = 2 (010). The bitwise AND operation is correct.

title: gets() Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: gets() was removed in C11 due to buffer overflow vulnerability. Use fgets() instead.

title: Union Memory Sharing
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The union members share memory. Setting and reading the same member 'i' is correct.

title: Function Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. A function pointer fp correctly points to 'add' and is called with arguments 3 and 4.

title: Do-While Loop
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is a valid do-while loop that prints 0 1 2 3 4.

title: Sizeof Char
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. sizeof(char) is guaranteed to be 1 by the C standard.

title: Modifying String Literal
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Modifying a string literal through a pointer causes undefined behavior. String literals are stored in read-only memory.

title: Memory Leak
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The allocated memory is never freed. After printf, there should be a free(p) call to avoid a memory leak.

title: Incorrect realloc Usage
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: If realloc fails, assigning its result directly to 'p' loses the original pointer, causing a memory leak. Use a temporary pointer.

title: Incorrect Linked List Insertion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Assigning to local parameter 'head' does not modify the caller's pointer. Need a double pointer (struct Node **head).

title: Wrong Precedence with Bitwise OR
description: Find the line with the logical error.
explanation: The '==' operator has higher precedence than '&'. The expression is parsed as 'flags & (mask == 0x0F)'. Use parentheses: '(flags & mask) == 0x0F'.

title: Incorrect String Length Calculation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Buffer overflow: 'dest' is 5 bytes but 'Hello' needs 6 bytes (including null terminator). dest should be at least char dest[6].

title: Incorrect Use of feof
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Using feof() as the loop condition reads one extra character. feof is only set AFTER a failed read. Check fgetc return value instead.

title: Signed/Unsigned Comparison
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Comparing signed and unsigned integers: 'b' is implicitly converted to unsigned, making -1 become a very large number. The else branch executes.

title: Off-by-One in String Copy
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: malloc(len) allocates strlen bytes but does not account for the null terminator. Should be malloc(len + 1).

title: Incorrect Recursive Base Case
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Calling fibonacci(0) has no base case. It will recurse with fibonacci(-1) and fibonacci(-2), causing a stack overflow.

title: Volatile Missing in Signal Handler
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The variable 'flag' should be declared as 'volatile sig_atomic_t'. Without volatile, the compiler may optimize away the loop check.

title: Incorrect Variadic Function
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Missing va_end(args) before return. Not calling va_end results in undefined behavior.

title: Dangling Pointer After Realloc
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: After realloc, 'b' may point to freed memory if realloc moved the block. Accessing b[0] is undefined behavior.

title: Incorrect Flexible Array Member
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A struct with a flexible array member must be dynamically allocated with extra space for the array. Stack allocation doesn't allocate space for data[].

title: Incorrect Bit Field Width
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A bit field of width 33 exceeds the size of 'unsigned int' (typically 32 bits). This is a compilation error.

title: strcpy to Overlapping Memory
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: strcpy does not handle overlapping memory regions. This is undefined behavior. Use memmove instead.

title: Incorrect Multithreading with Global
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Incrementing a shared global variable without a mutex or atomic operation causes a data race and undefined behavior.

title: Wrong Function Prototype
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The prototype 'int add()' in C means the function accepts an unspecified number of arguments, not zero. It should be 'int add(int a, int b)' to match the definition.

title: Missing include for malloc
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: malloc and free are declared in <stdlib.h> which is not included. This causes an implicit function declaration warning/error.

title: Use After Free
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Accessing arr[0] after free(arr) is use-after-free, which is undefined behavior.

title: Incorrect Token Paste in Macro
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The comment-based token pasting 'a/**/b' is not valid in modern C. Use the ## operator: #define CONCAT(a, b) a##b.

title: Incorrect sizeof for Dynamic Array
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: sizeof(arr) returns the size of the pointer (4 or 8 bytes), not the allocated memory. Cannot determine dynamic array size this way.

title: Incorrect Null Check After fopen
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: No null check after fopen. If the file doesn't exist, fp is NULL and passing it to fgets causes a crash.

title: Macro Side Effect
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Macro arguments with side effects (x++, y++) are evaluated multiple times. y gets incremented twice causing unexpected results.

title: Recursive Struct Definition
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A struct cannot contain an instance of itself (infinite size). It must use a pointer: 'struct Node *next'.

title: Strict Aliasing Violation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Casting a float pointer to an int pointer and dereferencing violates the strict aliasing rule, causing undefined behavior.

title: Designated Initializer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Designated initializers are valid in C99. arr[2] is 10, arr[4] is 20, and others are 0.

title: Compound Literal
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Compound literals are valid in C99. This creates a temporary struct Point and copies it to p.

title: Void Pointer Arithmetic
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Pointer arithmetic on void pointers is not allowed in standard C because the compiler doesn't know the element size. It is a GCC extension.

title: Inline Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Static inline functions are valid in C99. The function is correctly defined and called.

title: calloc Zero Initialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: calloc allocates memory and initializes all bytes to zero. arr[2] is guaranteed to be 0.

title: Restrict Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The restrict qualifier is valid in C99. It tells the compiler that dest and src don't overlap, enabling optimization.

title: Array Decay
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Arrays decay to pointers when passed to functions. This is valid and prints 1 2 3 4.

title: Const Correct String Return
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Returning a pointer to a string literal is valid since string literals have static storage duration. Using const char* is correct.

title: Macro Stringification
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The # operator in macros converts the argument to a string literal. This correctly prints 'Hello World'.

title: Negative Array Index
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Negative indices are valid in C when they refer to valid memory. p[-2] is equivalent to *(p - 2), which is arr[1] = 20.

title: Switch with String
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C switch statements only work with integer types. Strings cannot be used in switch/case labels.

title: Scope of Variables in Blocks
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The inner block creates a new scope, shadowing the outer 'x'. Output: '20 10'.

title: Hexadecimal Float Literal
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Hexadecimal floating-point literals are valid in C99. 0x1.8p1 = 1.5 * 2^1 = 3.0.

title: Goto Statement
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Although discouraged, goto is valid in C. This code correctly prints 0 1 2 3 4 using goto-based loop logic.

title: Struct Assignment
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Struct assignment is valid in C. It performs a shallow copy of all members. b.x = 1, b.y = 2.

title: strtol for String to Int
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. strtol correctly converts the string to a long integer in base 10.

title: Bitwise Left Shift Overflow
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Left shifting a signed 1 into the sign bit (bit 31) is undefined behavior in C for signed integers.

title: Array of Function Pointers
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Arrays of function pointers are valid in C. ops[2] points to mul, and mul(4,3) returns 12.

title: Implicit Return 0 from Main
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In C99 and later, if main reaches the closing brace without a return statement, it implicitly returns 0.

title: Ternary as Lvalue
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In C, the result of the ternary operator is not an lvalue (unlike in C++). You cannot assign to it.

title: Anonymous Struct in C11
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Anonymous structs are valid in C11. Members x and y can be accessed directly through 'o' without a named sub-struct.

title: Generic Selection Expression
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: _Generic is a C11 feature for type-generic expressions. 3.14 is a double, so it prints 'double'.

title: Static Assert
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: _Static_assert is valid in C11. It checks the condition at compile time. On most platforms, sizeof(int) is 4.

title: Thread Local Storage
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: thread_local (or _Thread_local) is valid in C11. Each thread gets its own copy of the variable.

title: Computed Goto
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Computed gotos (&&label and goto *ptr) are a GCC extension, not standard C. This is not portable C code.

title: Incorrect memcpy Size
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: memcpy copies bytes, not elements. Should be '5 * sizeof(int)' instead of '5' to copy all 5 integers correctly.

title: Buffer Overflow in snprintf
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While snprintf itself is safe, the return value 'n' (14) is larger than buffer size (10). Using n without checking for truncation is a logic error.

title: Incorrect qsort Comparator
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The comparator subtracts pointers instead of values. Should be '*(int*)a - *(int*)b' to compare the actual integer values.

title: Stack Buffer Used After Return
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Returning a pointer to the local stack buffer 'result'. The buffer is invalidated when concat returns.

title: Wrong Argument to sizeof in malloc
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: sizeof(Employee*) gives the size of a pointer (4/8 bytes), not the struct. Should be sizeof(Employee) to allocate enough memory.

title: Missing Struct Tag in Typedef
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The typedef name 'Node' is not available inside the struct definition. Self-referential structs need a tag: 'typedef struct Node { ... struct Node *next; } Node;'.

title: Incorrect Pointer Cast for Callback
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Casting a function taking 'const char*' to one taking 'int' and calling with an int is undefined behavior. The parameter types must match.

title: Missing Header for size_t
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While stdio.h often includes size_t, the proper header is <stddef.h> or <stdlib.h>. Relying on indirect inclusion is not portable.

title: Incorrect Endianness Assumption
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Assuming the first byte is 0x01 is not portable. On little-endian systems (most modern CPUs), bytes[0] would be 0x04.

title: Wrong Type for getchar Return
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: getchar() returns int, not char. EOF is typically -1, which can't be distinguished from a valid char if stored in a char variable.

title: Incorrect Array Initialization in Loop
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The outer loop uses 'i <= 3' instead of 'i < 3'. When i=3, matrix[3][j] is out of bounds access.

title: Unsequenced Modification
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Modifying 'i' more than once between sequence points is undefined behavior in C. The result is unpredictable.

title: Incorrect Multi-file Static Variable
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Declaring 'extern int count' inside printCount conflicts with the file-scope 'static int count'. Static variables have internal linkage; extern requests external linkage.

title: Wrong Order of Operations in Pointer
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While syntactically valid, *p++ is often misunderstood. It dereferences p first (val=10), then increments p. This is correct but commonly mistaken as incrementing the value.

title: Incorrect Conditional Compilation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: #ifdef checks if a macro is defined, not its value. DEBUG is defined (as 0), so 'Debug mode' always prints. Use '#if DEBUG' to check the value.

title: Misaligned Access via Packed Struct
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Taking the address of a potentially misaligned member in a packed struct and dereferencing it via int* can cause misaligned access on some architectures.

title: Incomplete Type in Function Parameter
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Forward declaring 'struct Node' in the function prototype before defining the struct creates an incomplete type at that scope, which may cause type mismatch errors.

title: Wrong Enum Comparison Type
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Comparing an enum value with a string literal "ON". Enum values are integers, not strings. Should compare with the enum constant: s == ON.

title: setjmp/longjmp Local Variable
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: After longjmp, local variables modified between setjmp and longjmp have indeterminate values unless declared volatile. 'x' should be 'volatile int x'.

title: Incorrect Linked List Free
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Accessing head->next after free(head) is use-after-free. Must save next pointer before freeing: 'struct Node *tmp = head->next; free(head); head = tmp;'.

title: Circular Include Guard Missing
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Including the same header twice without include guards causes a redefinition error for struct Config. Add #ifndef/#define/#endif guards.

title: Incorrect Bit Mask Check
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Due to operator precedence, this is parsed as '(perms & READ) && WRITE'. Should be '(perms & READ) && (perms & WRITE)' to check both flags.

title: Wrong Alignment with Union Trick
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Reading a different union member than the last one written is technically undefined behavior in C (though defined in C99 as a footnote). Also assumes sizeof(float) == sizeof(unsigned int).

title: Printf Format String Vulnerability
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Passing user input directly as the format string to printf is a format string vulnerability. An attacker can use %x, %n etc. Use printf("%s", user_input).

title: Recursive Macro Expansion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: FOO and BAR form mutually recursive macros. While the preprocessor won't infinitely expand (it stops on self-reference), the result isn't an integer, causing a compile error.

title: Compound Assignment Operator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: All compound assignment operators are valid. x = ((10+5-3)*2) = 24.

title: Pointer to Const vs Const Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: 'int * const p' is a const pointer to int. The pointer can't change, but the pointed-to value can. *p = 30 is valid.

title: Multiline String Macro
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Adjacent string literals are concatenated in C. The backslash continues the macro. MSG becomes "Hello World".

title: VLA in Struct
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Variable Length Arrays are not allowed as struct members in C. VLAs can only be used as local variables.

title: memset with Zero
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: memset with 0 correctly zeroes out the array. sizeof(arr) gives the total byte size. arr[5] is 0.

title: Array Name as Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Both *(arr+1) and 1[arr] are valid and equivalent to arr[1]. In C, a[b] is defined as *(a+b), so b[a] also works.

title: Complex Typedef
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: typedef for function pointers is valid. BinaryOp is a type for pointers to functions taking two ints and returning int.

title: Ternary with Different Types
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The ternary operator performs implicit type conversion. The int 1 is converted to double 1.0. result is 1.000000.

title: Concatenation of Macros
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is valid. The macro generates function definitions. #name stringifies the argument, and adjacent strings concatenate.

title: Bit Field Signed
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: A signed bit field of width 1 can only hold 0 or -1. Setting it to 1 overflows. The printed value is implementation-defined (likely -1).

title: Nested Function Calls
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nested function calls as arguments are valid. strlen("Hello")=5 + strlen(" World")=6 = 11.

title: Preprocessor Conditional with Undefined Macro
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In #if directives, undefined macros are replaced with 0. So UNDEFINED_MACRO == 1 becomes 0 == 1, which is false. The #error is skipped.

title: sizeof Expression Not Evaluated
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The expression inside sizeof is not evaluated (except for VLAs). x remains 5. sizeof(x++) returns sizeof(int). This is valid but tricky.

title: Zero-Length Array
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Zero-length arrays are a GCC extension, not standard C. Standard C99 uses flexible array members: 'char data[];' (no 0).

title: Switch Without Default
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: A switch without a default case is valid. If no case matches, execution continues after the switch. Output: ' Done'.

title: Long Long Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: long long is valid in C99+. The LL suffix and %lld format specifier are correct for long long values.

title: Complex Number
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C99 introduced native complex number support via <complex.h>. 'double complex' and the I macro are valid.

title: Boolean Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: stdbool.h provides bool, true, and false macros in C99. This is valid C99 code.

title: Infinite Recursion
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: While syntactically valid, f() calls itself without a base case, causing infinite recursion and a stack overflow at runtime.

title: Multi-character Constant
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Multi-character constants like 'AB' have implementation-defined values and are not portable. This is valid syntax but unreliable behavior.

title: Digraphs in C
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Digraphs are valid in C95+. <% is {, %> is }, <: is [, :> is ]. This is equivalent to using normal braces and brackets.

title: Void Pointer Assignment
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In C (unlike C++), void pointers can be implicitly converted to any other pointer type without a cast. This is valid.

title: Extern Variable Declaration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: extern declares a variable, then the next line defines it. This is valid since declaration and definition are in the same translation unit.

title: Casting malloc Return
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In C, casting malloc's return is unnecessary (void* implicitly converts). Not casting is actually the preferred style in C.

title: Register Keyword
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The register keyword is valid in C. It suggests (but doesn't guarantee) that the variable be stored in a CPU register. Taking its address is forbidden.

title: Circular Dependency in Headers
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: struct A uses a forward-declared pointer to struct B which is fine, but the overall design creates circular dependencies. However, the real issue is that struct A references struct B before B is defined, only as a pointer which is valid.

title: Incorrect Use of Restrict with Overlapping
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Passing the same array for both restrict parameters violates the restrict contract. The compiler assumes a and b don't alias, so results are undefined.

title: Wrong Use of Flexible Array with sizeof
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Copying a struct with a flexible array member using assignment only copies the fixed-size portion. The data[] member is not included in the copy.

title: Incorrect Atomic Operation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Load-then-store is not atomic as a whole. Between load and store, another thread can modify the value. Use atomic_fetch_add(&counter, 1) for a truly atomic increment.

title: Incorrect Struct Padding Assumption
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: memcmp on structs with padding is unreliable because padding bytes have indeterminate values. Also 'b' is uninitialized. Compare members individually instead.

title: Sequence Point in Function Args
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The order of evaluation of function arguments is unspecified in C. Modifying 'i' in multiple arguments is undefined behavior.

title: Incorrect Bit Rotation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Shifting by 32 bits (equal to the type width) is undefined behavior for 32-bit unsigned int. Should guard against n == 0 or n >= 32.

title: Incorrect Token Concatenation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: VAR(1+1) expands to var_1+1, not var_2. Token pasting doesn't evaluate expressions. This creates 'var_1 + 1' instead of 'var_2'.

title: Incorrect Comparison After Casting
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Casting 200 to signed char wraps it to -56 (on most systems). So (signed char)a = -56 < (signed char)b = 100, printing 'b >= a'.

title: Incorrect String Pool Assumption
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Comparing string literal pointers with == is unreliable. While compilers may pool identical literals (making a == b true), this is implementation-defined. Use strcmp().

title: Wrong Return Type in Callback Registration
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Assigning 'void (*)()' function to 'int (*)()' pointer is a type mismatch. The function returns void but the pointer expects int return.

title: Incorrect Use of Strtok
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: strtok modifies the string passed to it. Passing a string literal (read-only memory) causes undefined behavior. Use a char array instead.

title: Incorrect Sizeof with Pointer Decay
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Despite 'char buf[1024]' in the parameter, buf decays to a pointer. sizeof(buf) gives pointer size (8), not 1024. Pass the size separately.

title: Race Condition in File Operations
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: TOCTOU (Time-of-Check-to-Time-of-Use) race condition: the file could be deleted between stat() and fopen(). Should check fopen's return value.

title: Incorrect Null Pointer Check Pattern
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Should check 'head != NULL' not 'head->next != NULL'. If head itself is NULL, accessing head->next is undefined behavior. Also misses the last node.

title: Incorrect Use of Assert
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Using assert for malloc failure is wrong because asserts are disabled with NDEBUG in release builds. Use proper error handling (if/return) for runtime errors.

title: Wrong Order of Free in 2D Array
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Freeing the outer array first makes matrix[i] invalid. Must free inner arrays first, then free the outer array.

title: Implicit Function Declaration Side Effect
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: sqrt is used without including <math.h>. Without the prototype, the compiler assumes sqrt returns int, producing wrong results.

title: Incorrect Recursive Struct Size Calculation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: malloc(sizeof(struct Tree*)) allocates pointer size (8 bytes), not struct size. Should be sizeof(struct Tree) to allocate enough for the whole struct.

title: Buffer Overrun in strcat
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: greeting is 12 bytes, already holding 'Hello ' (7 bytes incl. null). Appending 'World!!!' (8 bytes) needs 15 bytes total, overflowing the buffer.

title: Modifying Const Through Pointer Cast
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Casting away const and modifying the original object that was declared const is undefined behavior. The compiler may optimize based on x being const.

title: Missing Return in Non-Void Function
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The function max does not return a value when a <= b. Falling off the end of a non-void function and using the return value is undefined behavior.

title: Wrong calloc Arguments
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: calloc arguments are swapped. It should be calloc(10, sizeof(int)) - count first, then size. While it works mathematically, the argument order is reversed from convention.

title: Incorrect Macro Guard
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The second #ifndef MY_HEADER_H block will never execute because MY_HEADER_H was already defined in the first block. struct Extra is never declared.

title: Incorrect Complex Linked List Reversal
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: After setting current->next = prev, current->next no longer points to the original next node. Must save next before overwriting: 'struct Node *next = current->next;' before the assignment.

title: Flexible Array Member with malloc
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is the correct way to use a flexible array member. Extra bytes after sizeof(struct Msg) are used for the text[] array.

title: Aligned Allocation
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: aligned_alloc is valid in C11. It allocates 256 bytes aligned to a 64-byte boundary. The size (256) is a multiple of alignment (64).

title: Recursive Struct with Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Self-referential structs with pointers are valid. Node b points to a, and b.next->data correctly accesses a.data (1).

title: Wide Character String
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Wide character strings with L prefix and wprintf with %ls are valid C. wchar.h provides the necessary declarations.

title: Constant Expression in Case
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Case labels require constant expressions. Macro-based arithmetic like BASE * 2 + 5 is evaluated at compile time and is valid.

title: Statement Expression (GCC)
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Statement expressions ({...}) and typeof are GCC extensions, not standard C. This code is not portable across compilers.

title: Null Pointer Constant
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Both 0 and (void*)0 are valid null pointer constants in C. Both p and q are null pointers and compare equal.

title: Variadic Macro
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Variadic macros with __VA_ARGS__ are valid in C99. The ## before __VA_ARGS__ (a GCC extension) handles the case with no extra arguments.

title: Offsetof Macro
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: offsetof is a standard macro from <stddef.h> that returns the byte offset of a member within a struct. This is valid and useful for understanding struct layout.

title: Nested Switch Statements
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nested switch statements are valid in C. Each break applies to its innermost enclosing switch.

title: Comma in Declaration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Multiple declarators of different forms (int, pointer to int, array of int) in one declaration line is valid in C.

title: Empty Struct
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Empty structs are not allowed in standard C (they are valid in C++ and GCC extension). A struct must have at least one named member.

title: Signal Handler Registration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Registering a signal handler with signal() is valid. Using _Exit (not exit) in a signal handler is safe since _Exit is async-signal-safe.

title: Array of Structs Initialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Initializing an array of structs with nested brace-enclosed initializer lists is valid C syntax.

title: Extern Inline Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In C99, 'extern inline' provides an extern definition. Without a separate non-inline definition in another TU, the linker may fail to find the symbol.

title: Mixed Declarations and Statements
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C99 allows mixing declarations and statements. Prior to C99, declarations had to come before any statements in a block.

title: Pointer Subtraction
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Pointer subtraction between elements of the same array is valid and returns the number of elements between them (3, not bytes). %td is correct for ptrdiff_t.

title: Atexit Registration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: atexit registers functions that run in LIFO order when the program exits. Output: 'Main Bye2 Bye1'.

title: Bitwise Complement
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The bitwise complement of 0x0F (00001111) is 0xF0 (11110000). The unsigned char stores it correctly as 0xF0.

title: Designated Struct Initializer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Designated struct initializers are valid C99. Order doesn't matter. Unspecified members (depth) are zero-initialized.

title: Compound Literal as Argument
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Compound literals can be passed directly as function arguments in C99. The temporary struct Point is created and passed by value.

title: Signed Right Shift
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Right shifting a negative integer is implementation-defined in C. The result depends on whether the compiler uses arithmetic or logical shift.

title: Complex Conditional Compilation
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nested preprocessor conditionals are valid. VERSION >= 3 is true, so FEATURE_X = 1, and the printf is compiled.

title: Container Of Macro Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The container_of pattern is valid and widely used (e.g., Linux kernel). It recovers the enclosing struct pointer from a member pointer using offsetof.

title: Atomic Type Qualifier
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C11 atomic types and operations are valid. _Atomic int, atomic_store, and atomic_load provide thread-safe access.
